chunks.text: Gerald Friedland is an Adjunct Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley EECS. He is a Principal Scientist in the AI/ML Lab at Amazon AWS, leading a group on systematic approaches to fully automatic, no-code machine learning and data science. Previously, he was CTO and co-founder of Brainome, Inc, which created a no-code machine learning tool. Before that, he was a Principal Data Scientist at Lawrence Livermore National Lab, integrating machine learning systems with traditional sciences, especially physics.

He had been with the International Computer Science Institute for 10 years, heading a video and speech analytics group. His accomplishments include being the lead figure behind the Multimedia Commons initiative, a collection of 100M images and 1M videos for research. He is the creator of the SIOX object segmentation tool, the de-facto open-source standard for semi-automatic image segmentation since 2005. He has published more than 200 peer-reviewed articles in conferences, journals, and books.

He recently authored a textbook on the intersection of information theory and machine learning, translated into several languages. He also co-authored a textbook on multimedia computing with Cambridge University Press. He served as associate editor for ACM Transactions on Multimedia and IEEE Multimedia Magazine and regularly reviews for several IEEE and Springer journals. He is the recipient of several research and industry recognitions, including the European Academic Software Award and the Multimedia Entrepreneur Award.

Dr. Friedland received his doctorate (summa cum laude) and master's degree in computer science from Freie Universitaet Berlin, Germany, in 2002 and 2006, respectively. His education includes a 2006 Ph.D. and a 2002 MSc in Computer Science from Freie Universitat Berlin. His research areas are Signal Processing (SP), Multimedia Computing, Artificial Intelligence (AI), and Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences (IDNCS).

He is affiliated with the Berkeley Institute for Data Science (BIDS) and the Center for Information Technology Research in the Interest of Society - The Banatao Institute (CITRIS). Selected publications include 'Information-Driven Machine Learning: Data Science as an Engineering Discipline' (Springer-Nature, 2024), 'YFCC100M: The New Data in Multimedia Research' (Communications of the ACM, 2016), 'Multimodal Location Estimation of Videos and Images' (Springer, 2014), and 'Introduction to Multimedia Computing' (Cambridge University Press, 2014).
